title: Efficient Acquisition of Short Tandem Repeats in Single-Cell Whole-Genome-Amplified DNA
author: Tao, Marx, et al.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: The protocol below describes the specific steps for using whole genome amplified genomic DNA (REPLI-g Mini Kit, Qiagen) from Du145 single cells for the 12K OM6 STR panel presented in our Cell Reports Methods paper (Tao etÂ al., 2021) (Custom Array). However, we have also used this protocol for primary cells such as melanoma, leukemia, T-cells, Macrophages, etc. and other whole genome amplification kits such as REPLI-g Single Cell Kit, Ampli1WGA kit, MALBAC single cell WGA kit etc.

steps.title: Duplex MIP preparation
steps.instruction: Prepare the duplex molecular inversion probes for a 12K panel of selected human STRs, OM6, to enrich these targets from the single cell WGA DNA in the following steps.1.KOD Hot Start Real Time Custom PCR Mix 5Ã (KOD 5Ã Custom Mix)a.Prepare SYBR 100Ã by mixing 10Â ÎỳL from stock SYBR green I (Lonza, 10,000Ã) and 990Â ÎỳL Dimethyl Sulfoxide (DMSO) (Sigma).b.Prepare 2Â mL KOD 5Ã Custom Mix according to the table below.ReagentsStock conc.Final conc.KOD 5Ã custom mix (Îỳl)ddH2O0.27KOD Buffer 10Ã (Merck)10Ã5Ã2.5MgSO4 25Â mM (Merck)25Â mM7.5Â mM1.5dNTP 25Â mM each (Bioline)25Â mM7.5Â mM0.2KOD Enzyme 1Â U/ÎỳL (Merck)1Â U/ÎỳL0.1Â U/ÎỳL0.5SYBR 100Ã (Lonza)100Ã1Ã0.025Total Volume52.PreAmp PCR (8 reactions)a.Dilute the synthesized oligo pool (Custom Array, Inc.) to 1Â ng/ÎỳL to prepare PCR template.b.Amplification primers designed to bind universal adapters are used for PreAmp PCR in LightCycler 480 (LC480, Roche) as shown below:OM4_Mly_F: GTCTATGAGTGTGGAGTCGTTGCOM4_Mly_R: CTAGCTTCCTGATGAGTCCGATGNote: SYBR in KOD 5Ã Custom Mix can be used to track the amplification for real time PCR.PreAmp PCR Mix:ReagentsStock conc.Final conc.1Ã PreAmp PCR mix (Îỳl)Template1Â ng/ÎỳL0.2Â ng/ÎỳL1.8OM4_Mly_F primer10 pmol/ÎỳL0.3 pmol/ÎỳL1.35OM4_Mly_R primer10 pmol/ÎỳL0.3 pmol/ÎỳL1.35KOD 5Ã Custom Mix5Ã1Ã9ddH2O31.5Total Volume45PreAmp PCR program:PCR cycling conditionsStepsTemperatureTimeCyclesInitial Denaturation95ÂḞC120 s1Denaturation95ÂḞC20 s18 cyclesAnnealing60ÂḞC10 sExtension70ÂḞC5 sFinal extension70ÂḞC50 s1Hold4ÂḞCForeverc.Purify PreAmp PCR product by MinElute PCR purification kit (Qiagen).d.Measure concentration by Qubit dsDNA HS Assay Kit (Life Technologies).3.Production PCR (48 reactions). Troubleshooting 3a.Dilute purified PreAmp PCR product to 1Â ng/ÎỳL for template.b.96 well plate production PCR is performed according to the setup below. Amplification is tracked by SYBR present in the KOD 5Ã Custom Mix.ReagentsStock conc.Final conc.1Ã production PCR (Îỳl)Template1Â ng/ÎỳL0.2Â ng/ÎỳL1.8OM4_Mly_F primer10 pmol/ÎỳL0.3 pmol/ÎỳL1.35OM4_Mly_R primer10 pmol/ÎỳL0.3 pmol/ÎỳL1.35KOD 5Ã Custom Mix5Ã1Ã9ddH2O31.5Total Volume45Production PCR programPCR cycling conditionsStepsTemperatureTimeCyclesInitial Denaturation95ÂḞC120 s1Denaturation95ÂḞC20 s12 cyclesAnnealing60ÂḞC10 sExtension70ÂḞC5 sFinal extension70ÂḞC50 s1Hold4ÂḞCForeverc.PCR product are pooled and purified by MinElute columns (Qiagen).d.Elute with 45Â ÎỳL ddH2O per column.e.Pool all purified products.f.Measure the DNA concentration of the final pool by loading 1Â ÎỳL of the pool onto a NanoDrop spectrophotometer (Thermo Scientific).g.Dilute the pool to âỳ30Â ng/ÎỳL based on measured concentration.h.Retain 20Â ÎỳL of sample to evaluate size distribution in Step 6. Carry the rest forward in Step 4.4.Digest the diluted DNA. Troubleshooting 4a.Combine diluted DNA with MlyI following the table belowReagentsStock conc.Final conc.1Ã with MlyI mix (Îỳl)Diluted DNA (30Â ng / uL)30Â ng/ÎỳL25.2Â ng/ÎỳL8410Ã NEB Smarter Buffer10Ã1Ã10MlyI10Â U/ÎỳL0.6Â U/ÎỳL6Total Volume100b.Incubate the mixture at 37ÂḞC overnight, deactivate at 80ÂḞC for 20Â min, and store at 4ÂḞC.5.Prepare final duplex MIP pool.a.Purify digested DNA by MinElute column.b.Pool elution samples into one tube.c.Measure concentration using by Qubit dsDNA HS (High Sensitivity) assay kit according to the manufacturer's protocol.6.Perform quality control on digested product size distribution. Run digested and undigested samples (Step 4b) on Tape Station (Agilent). The final duplex MIP pool should be âỳ105Â bp, and undigested sample from step (4b) should be âỳ150Â bp. (Figure1).FigureÂ 1Duplex MIPs quality control7.Based on length of 105Â bp and the concentration, the final duplex MIPs pool is diluted to 80Â nM (80 fmol/ÎỳL) stock solution, equivalent to 5.8Â ng/ÎỳL. Dilute further to 8Â nM as working solution. Store both stock and working solutions at â20ÂḞC.

steps.title: Whole-genome-amplified genomic DNA preparation
steps.instruction: Single-cell WGA DNA is prepared by selected kit in advance. Here we just describe thawing of the single cell WGA DNA for the following step.8.Clean the bench with 70% Ethanol. Take out a plate of whole genome amplified genomic DNA from â20 freezer.9.Thaw at room temperature.10.Shake on a bench top mixer, quickly spin down (approximately 30 s) at 500Â rpm.CRITICAL: Keep the plate well sealed to avoid cross contamination.

steps.title: Library preparation and sequencing
steps.instruction: Illumina sequencing adapters and unique barcode per cell are added by a barcoding PCR. Then all the samples are pooled into one tube in equal volume and then equal molecular concentration. The pools are size selected by Blue Pippin to remove dimmers and by products. library pools passed quality control are sequenced on MiSeq or NextSeq with default illumine sequencing primers.4.Sample specific barcoding PCRa.Note the structure of the dual-index Illumina barcoding primers used in the experiments:i.i5-index-primer: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5-8bp-index]ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCG;ii.i7-index-primer: CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT[i7-8bp-index]GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCG;b.2Â ÎỳL product from the previous step (step 3) are amplified with a pair of unique barcoding primers for each sample in a reaction as shown below.ReagentsStock conc.Final conc.1Ã (Îỳl)TemplateNANA2dual-index Illumina primers5 pmol/ÎỳL each0.5 pmol/ÎỳL each2NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix2Ã1Ã10SYBR 100Ã10Ã0.5Ã1ddH2O5Total Volume20Barcoding PCR programTemperatureTimeCycles98ÂḞC30 s98ÂḞC10 sÃ5 cycle56ÂḞC30 s65ÂḞC45 s98ÂḞC10 sÃ15 cycle65ÂḞC75 s65ÂḞC5Â min4ÂḞCHold5.Sample pooling and Purification for Diagnostic Sequencinga.Clean up barcoded PCR product in a 96-well plate using 0.8Ã AMPure XP SPRI magnetic beads (Beckman Coulter) according to manufactoryâs manual by Tecan liquid handling system, eluted in 40Â ÎỳL ddH2O.b.Pool equal volumes (usually take 2 ul) of purified samples manually.c.Concentrate the pool by MinElute according to manufacturer instructions, elute with 35Â ÎỳL ddH2O.6.Size Selection for Diagnostic Sequencinga.Retain 3 ul of the concentrated pool for quality control in step 5.b.Run 30Â ÎỳL of the concentrated pool on a lonza 2% V1 cassette BluePippin (Sage Science) with setting range 240â340Â bp according to manufactoryâs protocol. Agarose gel extraction in the range of 240â340Â bp can serve as an alternative.c.Purify size-selected elution by MinElute, elute with 15Â ÎỳL ddH2O.d.Measure concentration by Qubit dsDNA HS (High Sensitivity) assay kit. Troubleshooting 1e.Inspect size distribution of the concentrated pool before and after size selection using a Tape Station dsDNA chip (Figure 2 is a reuse of panel 1 in Supplementary Figure 1 from our Cell Reports Methods paper (Tao etÂ al., 2021) and confirms a single peak around 300Â bp. Troubleshooting 2FigureÂ 2Quality control of sequencing libraryf.Dilute size-selected pool to make 12Â ÎỳL of 4Â nM (4 fmol/ÎỳL) library for Illumina NGS calculated based on the concentration and average size reported by the Tape Station.7.Diagnostic sequencing (âỳ17Â h for sequencing, âỳ2Â h for analysis) Troubleshooting 5a.Sequence at 10 pM loading concentration. We recommend to run on a 300 cycle MiSeq Nano flow cell in pair end mode. Set Read1 and Read2 as 151, and both Index1 and Index2 reads as 8. Minimum read length we have tested is 125 Ã 2 pair end to allow sequencing through the repeat regions of most STRs in our design. Default sequencing primers suffice for sequencing.b.Following bcl2fastq demultiplexing, merge overlapping Read1 and Read2 with the following command:Â >pear -v 40 -m 300 -f fastq1 -r fastq2 -opear_files_prefixc.Map merged reads against customized STR reference (as shown in FigureÂ 3) of all amplicons with bowtie2, each appearing multiple times, once with every possible STR length.Â >bowtie2 -x index_files_prefix -U merged_fastq |samtools view -bS - | samtools sort -osorted_assignment_bamFigureÂ 3MS reads mappingEach read is mapped to a specific target locus according to its flanking regions.d.For more details, parallel execution and integration to the clineage analysis system, pleaseÂ seeÂ the codes at: https://github.com/shapirolab/clineage/blob/master/sequencing/analysis/full_msv/full_msv.pye.Extract the total number of reads per sample from âsorted_assignment_bamâ with pysam.8.Balancing reads per samplea.Calculate the scaling volume for each sample based on the total number of reads extracted from the diagnostic sequencing result to equalize the read coverage per sample. For example, sample A got 500 reads, sample B got 1000 reads in the diagnostic sequencing, to equalize the read coverage in the following production sequencing, we can pool 2 ul sample A with 1 ul sample B.b.According to the scaling volume, pool purified samples from step (5a) manually or by Echo550, then concentrate by miniElute, elute in 35ÎỳL ddH2O.c.Prepare production sequencing library for pooled samples as in step (6).9.Production sequencing (âỳ29Â h for sequencing)

steps.title: Library preparation and sequencing
steps.instruction: The minimum reads per samples is 1M, and the minimum read length is 125 Ã 2 pair end. We recommend to sequence up to 200 samples on one NextSeq500 high output flow cell with 151Ã2 pair-end run parameters according to manufactory manual and relying on default sequencing primers. Set both Index1 and Index2 as 8. Load at 1.8â2.2 pM concentration. (FigureÂ 3)Optional: If the production sequencing doesnât generate enough reads for some samples (i.e over 1M reads for samples enriched with the OM6 panel), another round of NextSeq could be conducted using the same library for these samples. Consider Hiseq or NovaSeq platforms for large scale projects.
